segments.text: So, today we will be covering from 5 to 12. So, this is the fifth verse. That knowledge of the

and effect. There is a verse which says, Shruti means, what is Shruti? Shruti means
hearing.
Scriptures are also called Shruti Shastras. Can anyone help me with an answer? Why they
are called Shruti Shastras? Because originally, this Vedic knowledge was imparted to the first
created being, Lord Brahma. He passed on to Narada by what? By what means? By hearing.
Brahma.
Then Narada heard. Then Narada heard, then Vyasa heard from Narada. So, this knowledge
was coming down in a Parampara. Parampara means Reciplic succession and that was through
oral reception. And therefore, our Shastras are also called Shruti. Only 5000 years back,
this Vedic knowledge was not imparted to the first created being. It was imparted to the
second created being. So, the Vedic knowledge which was coming down in Parampara was put
into writing. Otherwise, this knowledge was not in a written form. And Vedavyas compiled
the Vedic literatures, the four Vedas, Rig, Yajur, Atharva and Sama Veda. So, it is said
that Shruti, Smriti, Puranadi, there are Puranas. How many Puranas are there?
